titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the D. H. Lawrence Collection
titlestmt.author: Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11915613.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday October 3, 2020 at 03:52

did.unittitle: D. H. Lawrence collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, agreements and contracts, photographs and artwork. The material documents Lawrence's writing career, his relationships with friends and publishers, and his travels in New Mexico, Mexico and Europe. Included is correspondence between his wife, Frieda Lawrence, and their mutual friends, and records relating to the rights to D. H. Lawrence's works after his death.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired by gift and purchase, 1957-1989. For more information consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing, in 2014.
The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as MS Vault Shelves Lawrence, MS Vault Lawrence, Uncat MS Vault File, Uncat MS Vault 133, Uncat MS Vault 554.

The finding aid for this collection is partially compiled from individual catalog cards that were created at or around the time of receipt by the library. Information from the catalog cards was repurposed in the finding aid in accordance with current archival descriptive standards. Some catalog cards, copies of which are filed with the collection, include more detailed descriptions of individual items and/or provenance notes. Descriptions for items not represented by catalog cards were derived from accession records, folder labels, or other available descriptive material.

bioghist.head: D. H. Lawrence (1885-1930)
bioghist.p: D. H. (David Herbert) Lawrence was an English writer and painter.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The D. H. Lawrence Collection consists of correspondence, writings, agreements and contracts, and photographs and drawings. The material documents Lawrence's writing career, his relationships with friends and publishers, and his travels in New Mexico, Mexico and Europe. Included is correspondence between his wife, Frieda Lawrence, and their mutual friends, and records relating to the rights to D. H. Lawrence's works after his death.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into ten groupings: I. Pre-1960 Acquisitions. II. 1964 Acquisition. III. 17 January 1966 Acquisition. IV. 27 January 1966 Acquisition. V. September 1966 Acquisition. VI. August 1969 Acquisition. VII. December 1969 Acquisition. VIII. 1971 Acquistion. IX.1973 Acquisition. X. May 1989 Acquisition.

did.unittitle: Pre-1960 Acquisitions

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call numbers: MS Vault Lawrence, MS Vault Shelves Lawrence.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gifts of H. Bacon Collamore, 1957-1958; Dudley Nichols,1959; and the Yale Library Associates, 1958.

did.unittitle: Writings
"A Chapel Among the Mountains," autograph manuscript, 16 pages
"Clouds," autograph manuscript, 12 leaves, written in an exercise book, signed on back cover
"The Lovely Lady," typescript with corrections, 42 leaves
"Making Love to Music," autograph manuscript, 14 pages; with note probably addressed to Nancy Pearn of Curtis-Brown added at end
"The Novel: An Essay," typescript and autograph manuscript, 12 leaves
Poems: "Ruination," autograph manuscript; "Last Hours," "On the March," "Rondeau of a Conscientious Objector," and "Tommies in the Train," typescripts with autograph corrections, 7 leaves

"Sea and Sardinia," typescript with autograph corrections, 305 leaves; accompanied by an original map of Sardinia, drawn in ink, titled and initialed on verso by Lawrence, with an envelope addressed to Thomas Seltzer
"We Need One Another," "Nobody Loves Me," and "The Real Thing," autograph essays, signed, 17 pages written in an exercise book labelled "Three Essays for Vanity Fair"
D. H. Lawrence correspondence
Falk, Bernard
Mathews, Elkin
Pinker, J. B.
Savage, Henry
Seltzer, Thomas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written on verso of a photograph of Lawrence.

did.unittitle: Squire, Sir John
Wilson, Charles
Unidentified recipient
Frieda Lawrence correspondence with Dudley Nichols and his wife Esta

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph of Frieda Lawrence and Robinson Jeffers enclosed with letter dated July 18, 1936. Some letters have postscripts by Angelo Ravagli, Frieda Lawrence's husband

did.unittitle: Angelo Ravagli correspondence with Dudley and Esta Nichols

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter dated August 23, 1956 describing the death of his wife Frieda.

did.unittitle: Photograph of Lawrence by Nickolas Muray
"The Finding of Moses" oil painting
1964 Acquisition

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Fault File.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter signed from D. H. Lawrence to James B. Pinker; with typed transcript
January 17,1966 Acquisition

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Willard Johnson on the William Robertson Coe Fund, George B. Alvord Fund, and the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 1966.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat Ms Vault 133.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings by D. H. Lawrence, Alice Long, and Mabel Dodge Luhan; correspondence between D. H. and Frieda Lawrence and Willard (Spud) Johnson and others; photographs of D. H. and Frieda Lawrence and friends; a drawing of D. H. Lawrence by Witter Bynner; and printed material regarding the death of D. H. Lawrence.

did.unittitle: "Just Back from the Snake Dance," manuscript on verso of letter to D. H. and Frieda Lawrence
"Mr. McKinley he aint' done no wrong," manuscript verse; with "The Cherry Tree," manuscript verse on verso
"The Nightingale," manuscript essay
Typescript about D. H. Lawrence by Mabel [Dodge Luhan]
Typescript carbon letter, signed, to "Chère Jeunesse" with cover note from Lawrence to Willard Johnson
Letters, manuscript signed, to Willard (Spud) Johnson from D. H. Lawrence
Letters, manuscript, to Willard Johnson from Frieda Lawrence
Telegram from John H. McGinnis and George Bond to Spud Johnson
Letters to Willard Johnson from the Centaur Book Shop and Curtis Brown Ltd.
Note by unidentified sender to unidentified recipient
"Drawing of Lorenzo" by Witter Bynner, pen-and-ink drawing
Photographs of D. H. and Frieda Lawrence and friends
"To D. H. Lawrence," manuscript poem by Alice Long
Printed material about D. H. Lawrence, including issues of The Carmelite from March 1930
January 27, 1966 Acquisition

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: MS Vault Lawrence.

did.unittitle: Autograph letter, signed by D. H. Lawrence to "Chère Jeunesse"
September 1966 Acquisition

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault File.

did.unittitle: Three autograph letters signed from D. H. Lawrence to James B. Pinker
August 1969 Acquisition

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: MS Vault Shelves Lawrence.

did.unittitle: Pen and ink sketch of D. H. Lawrence by Willard (Spud) Johnson
December 1969 Acquisition

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Professor and Mrs. Cleanth Brooks, 1969.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault File

did.unittitle: Autograph letter signed from D. H. Lawrence to Gordon H. Campbell, accompanied by typed carbon transcript and a typed letter signed from Sam A. Eisenstein to Cleanth Brooks about the letter with his typed carbon reply
1971 Acquisition

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: MS Vault Lawrence.

did.unittitle: Two autograph letters signed from D. H. Lawrence to Henry Savage
1973 Acquisition

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased on the Edwin J. Beinecke Fund, 1973.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: MS Vault Lawrence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Volumes containing letters, postcards, writings, and photographs from D. H. and Frieda Lawrence to Catherine Carswell and her husband Donald Carswell.

did.unittitle: Volume I of letters and postcards from D. H. Lawrence and Frieda Lawrence to Catherine Carswell

Volume II of letters and postcards from D. H. Lawrence and Frieda Lawrence to Catherine Carswell; with an autograph manuscript of a theme for a novel to be written by D. H. Lawrence and Catherine Carswell, and 5 photographs of paintings by D. H. Lawrence
Volume containing typed transcripts of letters and novel theme in Volumes I and II
May 1989 Acquisition

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, agreements, copyright registrations, photographs and other material relating to the publication of D. H. Lawrence's books in the United States. Represented in the papers are the firms of Thomas Seltzer, Inc.; Albert and Charles Boni, Inc.; Robert M. McBride and Company, Publishers; Mitchell Kennerley; and Little, Brown & Company, Inc. Correspondence of D. H. and Frieda Lawrence with these publishers is also included.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call number: Uncat MS Vault 554.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Book Block on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 1989.

did.unittitle: Manuscript, typescript, and printed agreements, correspondence, and other documents relating to the publishing and copyright of Lawrence works
Copies of collection catalog cards
